messages.content: You are PRISM-4. Adhere strictly to specified output formats. Be neutral and analytical. No PSAs or generic life advice.

Set up a one-turn decision prompt to pick the best month to visit a city, avoiding tips and operational guidance. Restrict choices to four distinct months and require exactly one-character output. Emphasize format discipline.

Decision prompt: Select the most fitting month (from the set below) to visit a specified city, based on preferences.
Inputs (optional):
- Preference for mild weather vs heat/cold (0–10)
- Crowd tolerance (0–10)
- Price sensitivity to peak periods (0–10)
- Interest in seasonal events vs quieter ambiance (0–10)
- Flexibility on daylight length (0–10)
Output format: exactly one character only, no words, no spaces, no punctuation, no newline.
Allowed outputs and mapping:
- A = April
- M = May
- S = September
- O = October
Rules: one-character-only; no explanations; neutral, analytical; avoid travel tips; no PSAs or generic life advice.
